---
title: "Modelling Workflow"
---

The model workflow separates fixed assumptions, scenario parameters, derived parameters, optimisation variables, and outputs.

## Fixed Inputs

Fixed inputs remain constant across the compared architectures unless explicitly varied in a sensitivity case. Examples include:

- wind resource and Weibull distributions
- distance to shore
- water depth
- component cost assumptions
- efficiency and availability assumptions
- financing parameters
- source-specific cost curves

## Scenario Parameters

Scenario parameters define the case being evaluated. Current scenario dimensions include:

- sea area
- number of turbines
- export architecture: electricity, centralised hydrogen, or decentralised hydrogen
- hydrogen stack overplanting
- hydrogen pipeline pressure
- hydrogen pipeline selection

## Derived Parameters

Derived parameters are calculated deterministically from fixed inputs and scenario parameters. Examples include:

- turbine positions
- infield cable or pipeline length
- wake losses
- production profile
- stack operating point and efficiency
- balance-of-plant losses
- compressor power demand
- peak export capacity requirement

## Outputs

The principal outputs are yield and LCOE. The encyclopedia does not document result values, but it documents the assumptions needed to reproduce them:

- annual delivered energy in MWh/year
- areal yield in MWh/km2/year
- LCOE in EUR/MWh or EUR/kg-equivalent reporting where needed
- component-level annualised cost contribution
- optimised component sizing decisions
